methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $27M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 2 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Astro Mechanica is a Series A stage company in the Supersonic & Hypersonic sector, founded by Ian Brooke, with $27M+ in total capital raised to date. The comp universe for this sector is limited, consisting of 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 2 private peers (Boom Supersonic, Hermeus), reflecting the nascent state of the commercial hypersonics market.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to insufficient comparable data. The Stage-Progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $27M raised proxy, which represents typical valuation progression between Series A and subsequent rounds in early-stage hardware. This yields an implied valuation of $54M. A peer-median approach was not applicable; while two same-sector peers exist, neither the narrow filters (same stage, adjacent stage, similar-tier) nor the full-sector set produced the minimum three datapoints required for reliable comparable multiples without introducing substantial bias.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $46M to $62M, centered on a $54M median. This range reflects uncertainty bands around the single triangulation method rather than convergence across multiple approaches. The primary valuation risk stems from the sector's thinness—the lack of recent Series A transactions in supersonic/hypersonic technology limits confidence in stage-progression multiples. Resolution of this uncertainty requires either additional comparable transactions or clearer visibility into Astro Mechanica's commercial traction and technical milestones relative to peers.
